sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The pagerank_metric class is a specialization of the metric_template and is designed to perform PageRank calculations on graph structures. Although the metric implementation is incomplete, this documentation describes the core PageRank algorithm implemented in the class.

sect1.title: The pagerank_metric Class
sect1.para: The pagerank_metric class inherits from metric_template.

listitem.para: pagerank_metric(): The constructor initializes the metric name to "pagerank".
pagerank_metric* clone(): Creates a new instance of the pagerank_metric.
void pagerank(std::map<int, std::map<std::string, std::string>>* clust, std::map<int, std::map<int, float>>* bin_data): This method implements the PageRank algorithm.

sect2.title: PageRank Algorithm
sect2.para: The pagerank method takes a cluster of nodes (clust) and a matrix of binary edge data (bin_data) as input and calculates the PageRank for each node in the cluster.

The algorithm proceeds as follows:
Initialization:
The damping factor alpha is set to 0.85.
The transition matrix Mij is constructed from bin_data. The matrix is normalized such that the columns sum to 1.
The initial PageRank vector pr_ is initialized with the diagonal elements of bin_data, representing self-links.
Iteration:
The algorithm iteratively updates the PageRank vector PR until convergence.
In each iteration, the new PageRank pr_ is calculated as: pr_[i] = alpha * sum(Mij[i][j] * PR[j]) + (1 - alpha) / n_nodes.
The convergence is checked by comparing the norm of the difference between the new and old PageRank vectors.
Final Scores:
After convergence, the final PageRank scores are calculated based on the contributions from the other nodes in the graph.

simplesect.para: The define_metric, event, batch, and end methods are currently not implemented.
